stories.title: Senate confirms Sen. Mullin as DHS secretary. And, Iran denies U.S. talks to end war
stories.description: The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.

stories.title: Iran fires more missiles at Israel, dismisses Trump's talk as 'fake news'
stories.description: Israeli health officials said Iranian missiles struck four sites across Israel Tuesday, including central Tel Aviv, injuring at least six people. Iranian authorities also said a gas supply line in southwest Iran was struck overnight.

stories.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
stories.description: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

stories.title: Airstrikes may have destroyed Iran's last F-14s, ending a long, strange saga
stories.description: The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.

stories.title: How weeks of war have reshaped Iran's negotiating position
stories.description: How has Iran's negotiating position changed after weeks of war? NPR's A Martinez talks to Mohammad Ali Shabani, editor of the London-based news site Amwaj.media.

stories.title: UK police probe possible Iran link after Jewish charity ambulances set on fire
stories.description: Police in London are investigating a suspected antisemitic hate crime attack after four vehicles belonging to a Jewish ambulance service were set on fire.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Israel to expand ground and air attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: There are fears in Lebanon that Israel is preparing for a large-scale ground invasion.

stories.title: Iranian missiles injure 180 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
stories.description: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: Why there's no sign of the conflict in the Middle East 'winding down'
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister said that it doesn't appear that the US is prepared to halt its aggression after President Trump said the US was considering "winding down" its war.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as energy prices soar
stories.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

stories.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
stories.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

stories.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
stories.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

stories.title: 'You can't smell Nowruz in the air': Iran marks Persian new year under threat of strikes
stories.description: Iranians speak to the BBC about preparing for the Nowruz new year festival during the war.

stories.title: Are US and Israel aligned on Iran war? Deciphering Trump's post after gas field attacks
stories.description: What does Trump's Truth Social post after gas field attacks tell us about US-Israeli alignment?

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: 'It's been bittersweet': The Iranian-Iraqi cafe drawing crowds to Hackney
stories.description: Logma, a newly opened Iranian-Iraqi cafe in Hackney, has gone viral for its sandwiches.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: Tehran calls Trump's talk claims 'fake news' to reassure markets
stories.description: Tehran has called Donald Trump's claim of ongoing talks 'fake news' to reassure markets. This comes as the US president tries to get the Strait of Hormuz reopened, whose closure is shaking global markets. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle tells us more about what to know.

stories.title: Nigerian refinery begins fuel exports to Africa
stories.description: In tonight's edition: Nigeria's Dangote Refinery says it's stepped up exports across Africa as the war in Iran squeezes traditional fuel supply routes and disrupts energy flows. Also, 76 percent of births in sub-Saharan Africa occur in healthcare facilities without basic water, s...

stories.title: Increase in hostilities between israel and Hezbollah shows 'no sign of slowing down'
stories.description: An Israeli strike on Bshamoun, a town south of Beirut, killed at least two people. It also continued strikes on the southern suburbs of the capital. Since the start of Israeli strikes on the country, at least 1,039 people have been killed. FRANCE 24's Claire Paccalin is in Beirut...

stories.title: EU, Australia seal free trade deal eight years in the making
stories.description: The EU and Australia have signed a sweeping free trade agreement that will eliminate tariffs on nearly all trade between them as global economies seek to hedge against chaotic US tariff policies. Also in this edition: crude oil prices rise again as traders remain sceptical of sig...

stories.title: Unclear if Trump is telling truth about talks with Iran or if it's 'bluff'
stories.description: US President Donald Trump has said that the US and Iran are having 'very good' talks regarding the war. But they were denied by Tehran, although they did say they had received a request from the White House. There is also the question of where talks would be held - with Pakistan ...

stories.title: For Israel, war against Iran is not over as Trump's attempt to reach deal
stories.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israel would be ready to protect its vital interests under any circumstances, while it continues to strike Iran and Lebanon. This comes as US President Donald Trump has claimed talks were underway with Iran - despite Tehran denying t...

stories.title: Middle East live: Israel says it 'will control' security zone up to Lebanon's Litani river
stories.description: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said Tuesday that the country’s military “will control” southern Lebanon all the way to the Litani river. Katz said that Israeli forces had blown up “all five bridges over the Litani that were used by Hezbollah for the passage of terrorists an...

stories.title: Iran exploiting Trump administration's sensitivity to pressure from markets, expert says
stories.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Monte Francis, Nicholas Mulder, Assistant Professor at Cornell University and author of "The Economic Weapon: The Rise of Sanctions as a Tool of Modern War", explains that "the Trump administration is particularly sensitive to pressure from the markets" ...

stories.title: Amazon says AWS Bahrain region ‘disrupted’ following drone activity
stories.description: The disruption marks the second time in a month that AWS operations have been affected by the Iran war.

stories.title: Iran launches waves of missiles towards Israel
stories.description: Israel’s Magen David Adom emergency medical service said 'six people were lightly injured' in the missile strikes.

stories.title: Iran says no talks with US, calls Trump statements a ‘bluff’
stories.description: Iranian officials have dismissed statements by Trump as a ‘bluff’, denying any direct or indirect talks with the US.

stories.title: Drone footage shows aftermath of Iranian missile attack in Tel Aviv
stories.description: Drone footage shows extensive damage to a multi-storey residential building in Tel Aviv after a wave of Iranian attacks.

stories.title: Deadly air attack hits pro-Iran Popular Mobilisation Forces in Iraq
stories.description: Video shows the aftermath of an airstrike in Iraq’s Anbar province on a headquarters of the Popular Mobilisation Forces.

stories.title: Al Jazeera reports from scene of attack on Tehran residential area
stories.description: Al Jazeera reports from scene of attack on Tehran residential area

stories.title: Despite Trump’s peace talk claims, US-Israeli attacks continue to hit Iran
stories.description: Huge explosions were heard in Tehran and other cities, as Iran denied it held talks with the US to end the war.

stories.title: Top EU official calls for negotiations with Iran and an end to war
stories.description: European Commission President Ursula Von der Leyen has called for negotiations with Iran and an end to hostilities.

stories.title: Where do reported US-Iran ‘negotiations’ leave Israel?
stories.description: Israeli analysts describe confusion as US President Trump unexpectedly shifts from threatened strikes on Iran to talks.

stories.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 25 of attacks?
stories.description: Trump claims talks on with Iran, as he holds off on energy attacks, but Tehran denies any negotiations.

stories.title: North Korea’s Kim vows ‘irreversible’ nuclear status, warns Seoul of ‘merciless’ response
stories.description: North Korea will never give up nuclear weapons, leader Kim Jong Un said, indicating that it will soon designate South Korea the “most hostile state”, according to a state media report on Tuesday. Kim also told the country’s legislature in a policy address on Monday that the Unite...

stories.title: Australia, European Union agree sweeping new trade pact 8 years in the works
stories.description: The European Union (EU) and Australia struck a long-awaited free-trade deal on Tuesday, while also agreeing to boost defence cooperation and access to crucial rare-earth minerals in the face of global uncertainty over trade. EU chief Ursula von der Leyen’s visit to Australia come...

stories.title: US journalists sue to protect Voice of America from political control
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Journalists at the US government-funded broadcaster Voice of America (VOA) have filed a lawsuit accusing the Trump administration of undermining the outlet’s independence and turning it into a tool for political messaging. The complaint, filed on Monday in the US Dist...

stories.title: US deploys ICE agents at 14 airports
stories.description: WASHINGTON: The US immigration agency at the centre of a firestorm over heavy-handed enforcement tactics began deploying to major airports on Monday, as officials scrambled to ease mounting travel disruption during a prolonged, partial government shutdown. The move places Immigra...

stories.title: Pakistan in the limelight as US, Iran ‘open backchannel’
stories.description: • Efforts alongside Egypt and Turkiye seen as key to pausing threatened US strikes on Iran’s energy infrastructure• Administration sources say diplomatic efforts saw Islamabad leveraging its position as ‘net regional stabiliser’• PM speaks to Pezeshkian, Dar calls Araghchi; White...

stories.title: Iran wants US to provide it with solid peace guarantees — newspaper
stories.description: According to the report, Tehran also seeks to lift certain sanctions

stories.title: Hezbollah fires over 30 rockets at northwestern Israel — TV
stories.description: According to the TV channel, most of the rockets were intercepted by Israel’s Iron Dome air defense system

stories.title: Non-proliferation regime may be breached due to US-Israeli attacks on Iran — diplomat
stories.description: According to Mikhail Ulyanov, the non-proliferation regime is quite sustainable, "despite all the existing problems"

stories.title: US move to postpone strikes on Iran’s energy sites means no end to hostilities — newspaper
stories.description: According to The New York Times, the US and Israel are "continuing to carry out other strikes against Iran"

stories.title: World Athletics lifts all doping-related sanctions on RusAF — sports minister
stories.description: According to Mikhail Degtyarev, all of the set 34 strategic conditions and operational requirements have been fulfilled, and new management standards have been introduced

stories.title: US operation on Kharg Island could awaken Iran's sleeping forces — expert
stories.description: Marat Zembatov also did not rule out the possibility that the information about the operation on Kharg Island could be a US disinformation tactic

stories.title: Russian envoy points to Iran’s right to decide about IAEA’s access to Bushehr nuke plant
stories.description: Mikhail Ulyanov warned against comparing the situations around the Zaporozhye and Bushehr nuclear power plants

stories.title: Russia 'negative' on Iran conflict spilling over to Caspian Sea region — Kremlin
stories.description: Dmitry Peskov declined to comment on media reports alleging that Israel had carried out strikes on Russian ships in the Caspian Sea

stories.title: US, Israel continue attacks on Tehran, eyewitnesses told TASS
stories.description: According to the source, there were strikes at night and during the day

stories.title: IDF reports striking over 3,000 targets in Iran since start of operation
stories.description: Another series of strikes was conducted overnight on Tuesday as more than 50 installations were targeted

stories.title: Iran continues to face internet blackout for 24th day in row — NetBlocks
stories.description: The United States and Israel launched a military operation against Iran on February 28, when major Iranian cities, including Tehran, were struck

stories.title: White House press secretary describes situation around talks with Iran as fluid
stories.description: Karoline Leavitt said these are sensitive diplomatic discussions, and the United States will not negotiate through the press

stories.title: US undermines allied relations with Gulf nations over Iran — expert
stories.description: According to Marcus Vinicius De Freitas, regional economies have also begun to lose investment attractiveness

stories.title: Saudi Arabia, UAE may join conflict against Iran — newspaper
stories.description: According to The Wall Street Journal, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and other regional countries are "unified" in their discontent with Iran

stories.title: Oil traders ramp up bets ahead of US President’s Iran talks update — FT
stories.description: According to the publication, approximately 6,200 contracts for Brent and West Texas Intermediate crude oil were traded

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
